title: Separate Bride/Groom Photo Uploads & Fix Home Tab Layout Issues

requirements.text: In the Couple Details step (CoupleDetailsStep.tsx) and the Invitation Editor's couple details tab (InvitationEditor.tsx), replace the single 'couple photo URL' input field with two separate file upload inputs — one labeled 'Bride Photo' and one labeled 'Groom Photo'. Each upload should allow the user to select an image file, show a preview thumbnail, and store the uploaded photo independently. The InvitationFormContext must be updated to hold separate bridePhoto and groomPhoto fields instead of a single couplePhoto field. The backend Invitation type in main.mo must be updated to store bridePhoto and groomPhoto as separate optional Text fields. The guest-facing invitation pages (HeroSection, CoupleIntroduction) must render both photos side by side where a couple photo is currently displayed.

quotes: replace the single couple photo URL field with separate upload options for Bride and Groom photos

acceptanceCriteria: CoupleDetailsStep shows two distinct upload controls: 'Bride Photo' and 'Groom Photo', with no single 'couple photo URL' field present
Each upload control accepts an image file, displays a preview thumbnail after selection, and allows removal
InvitationFormContext stores bridePhoto and groomPhoto as separate fields
Backend Invitation record stores bridePhoto and groomPhoto as separate optional Text fields
Guest invitation page renders both the bride and groom photos side by side where applicable
Existing invitations with a legacy single couple photo field do not crash

requirements.text: On the Dashboard page (Dashboard.tsx), fix the layout so that invitation management controls — including the Publish button and any similar action buttons — remain fully visible and accessible at all times, including while an invitation is in an editing state. No action button should be hidden, clipped, or obscured based on editing state.

quotes: options like Publish (or similar controls) are getting hidden while editing

acceptanceCriteria: The Publish button and all other invitation action controls on the Dashboard (Home tab) are always visible regardless of whether the user is currently editing an invitation
No invitation card action is clipped or hidden by z-index, overflow, or conditional rendering tied to edit state

requirements.text: Remove or conditionally hide the 'Create Invitation' call-to-action text/button from pages where it does not belong. It should only appear on the Landing page and Dashboard page. It must NOT appear inside the invitation editor, the create wizard steps, or the guest-facing invitation page. Also fix the Header component (Header.tsx) so its 'Create Invitation' CTA does not render on editor or wizard routes, preventing it from overlapping other page sections.

quotes: the text 'Create Invitation' is appearing on every page and overlapping other sections

acceptanceCriteria: 'Create Invitation' text/button does not appear on the InvitationEditor page
'Create Invitation' text/button does not appear on CreateInvitationWizard pages
'Create Invitation' text/button does not appear on the GuestInvitation page
No visual overlap or layout displacement caused by the 'Create Invitation' element on any page other than Landing and Dashboard
The Header CTA is hidden on editor and wizard routes

constraints: Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths: useInternetIdentity.ts, useActor.ts, main.tsx, and frontend/src/components/ui
All Motoko logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor file
Preserve all existing invitation data and CRUD functionality; do not break existing queries or mutations
nonGoals: Redesigning theme templates to look more premium (deferred to a separate request)
Resizing or refining the RSVP section layout (deferred to a separate request)
Fixing the existing invitation event-loading bug ('No event created' while editing) — not included in this request scope
